results.title: MonoCloud for Startups: One identity layer for your customers, APIs, and agents | Product Hunt
results.content:
We are building with 10 design partners across SaaS and AI companies right now, and their feedback is shaping what ships next. If identity is core to what you are building and you want to shape where this goes, we are opening a few more design partner spots. Leave a comment here, or reach me on LinkedIn, and I will follow up.

👉 For the Product Hunt community, startups get the full MonoCloud platform free for one year, every premium feature, no gates. Apply here:  👈

We will be here all day. The one thing I would really love your feedback on: how are you handling identity for AI agents right now, and where does it break first? That is the piece we are building hardest, and honest answers help us more than any upvote.

I am happy to get into anything, the product or the roadmap.

Shivangi Tripathi

MonoCloud for Startups

MonoCloud for Startups

Maker

📌

Hey Product Hunt! 👋 Shivangi here, building MonoCloud with our founder Vishal @monocloud.

Auth always looks finished the day login works, but it never is. A customer asks for SSO. Your services need machine-to-machine keys. RBAC roles stop being able to express what you actually need. And now AI agents are acting on behalf of your users, and nobody can say what they are allowed to touch. Each piece gets bolted on separately, and a year later "auth" is five half-built tools held together with glue. [...] Report

Vishal Rastogi

MonoCloud for Startups

MonoCloud for Startups

Maker

Hey Product Hunt 👋 Vishal here, founder of MonoCloud.  
Every company is racing to put AI agents into production. Almost none of them can answer one simple question. When an agent does something it should not, who is accountable?

The log will say an agent did it. But an agent has no intent. Someone gave it that access, and today most teams cannot say who, cannot prove it, and cannot pull it back fast. That gap is the biggest unsolved problem in identity right now, and it is why I built MonoCloud.

results.title: MonoCloud for Startups — Developer Tools — Visalytica
results.content:
MonoCloud for Startups offers a comprehensive identity and access management platform designed for modern digital ecosystems. Unlike typical login solutions, it extends beyond authentication to provide detailed authorization, accountability, and control over user, service, and AI agent activities. The platform integrates fine-grained Cedar authorization, passkeys, SSO, API protection, machine-to-machine (M2M) communication, and mTLS with certificate-bound trust—all within a single, unified platform. Startups benefit from a full-featured security infrastructure that scales with their growth, with the added advantage of free access for one year. This makes MonoCloud particularly appealing for early-stage companies seeking robust security without immediate high costs. Its focus on both human [...] ## Screenshots

results.title: MonoCloud - Developer platform for secure authentication, API access, and agent identity
results.content: MonoCloud is a developer platform for secure authentication, API access, service identity, and agent access. Most startups outgrow basic auth once their product adds APIs, services, distributed workloads, or agent workflows. MonoCloud brings these identity layers into one platform so teams do not have to stitch together separate tools as they scale. → Add secure authentication with SSO, MFA, passkeys, social login, magic links, and passwordless flows. → Manage users, organizations, sessions, access, and consent from one identity layer. → Protect APIs with OAuth/OIDC, token introspection, opaque tokens, and session-bound access tokens. → Secure service identity with mTLS, certificate-bound tokens, and PKI integrations. → Manage identity and access for AI agents, autonomous workflows, and [...] strong security with a smooth experience. You get one less attack surface to worry about. Phishing resistant sign-in. No password to steal. 👉 Try MonoCloud free at monocloud[dot]com #Authentication #DeveloperTools #SaaS #Passkeys [Likes: 5, Comments: 0]; MonoCloud (1w): When you ask who decided an agent could do something, "it inherited the permission" is a dangerous answer. Most agent access today inherit the users permissions, there are no chains that anybody can trace. When a security team asks what an agent is allowed to do, the honest answer is whatever the user is allowed to do. Agent permissions should be explicit instead. Rules written per agent, in a form a human can actually read: which systems it can touch, which actions it can take, and what it must never do, all under its own [...] token is produced. The governance happens first. The token comes second. Catching a problem after the fact is auditing. Deciding before the fact is control. #AgentIdentity #Authorization #Cedar #AIAgents [Likes: 7, Comments: 0]; MonoCloud (1w): Phishing attacks steal passwords. There is no password for a passkey. Passkeys are phishing-resistant, passwordless credentials tied to a user's device. There is no password to intercept and no credential to leak in a data breach. Authentication uses the device's own unlock - a fingerprint, face scan, or PIN. The user experience is equally strong. One gesture, done. No typing, no remembering, no resetting. MonoCloud supports passkeys for seamless, phishing-resistant sign-ins. Your users get strong security with a smooth experience. You get one less
